title_original: Xanadu reveals government funding talks as company woos SPAC investors ahead of merger vote - The Globe and Mail
summary_original: Xanadu Quantum Technologies Inc. is in negotiations with the Ontario and Canadian federal governments for up to $390 million in funding, aiming to support advanced semiconductor and photonic manufacturing. This announcement comes as Xanadu approaches a critical shareholder vote on its merger with the United States-listed Crane Harbor Acquisition Corp., a deal that could yield up to US$455 million in net cash and would make Xanadu the first Canadian tech firm to go public on the Toronto Stock Exchange since 2021. Private backers, including AMD and major Canadian asset managers, have committed US$275 million. Xanadu seeks to fund its goal of building a quantum computer-based data centre by 2030 and continues to advance quantum error correction and modular optical quantum computing.
